summarization.a: The line chart shows the film thickness of various materials (TiN, Al₂O₃, Si₃N₄, HfO₂, ZrO₂, SiO₂) over cycles at 250°C using O₃ & HF.

a.question: Which material is selectively etched using O₃/HF chemistry at 250°C?
a.answer: Only TiN is etched; Al₂O₃, HfO₂, ZrO₂, SiO₂, and Si₃N₄ show negligible thickness change.

a.question: Why are Al₂O₃, HfO₂, and ZrO₂ not etched by O₃/HF despite being etchable by fluorination and ligand-exchange?
a.answer: These oxides form stable, nonvolatile fluorides (AlF₃, HfF₄, ZrF₄) when exposed to HF. Unlike TiN which forms volatile TiF₄, these metal fluorides remain on the surface and do not desorb, preventing net material removal in the oxidation-fluorination mechanism.

a.question: What is the approximate EPC for TiN etching shown in this figure?
a.answer: Approximately 0.20 Å/cycle, calculated from roughly 55 Å removal over about 300 cycles.

a.question: Why is selective TiN etching important for semiconductor fabrication?
a.answer: TiN is used as gate electrode material in CMOS and as a copper diffusion barrier, requiring selective removal without damaging surrounding dielectric materials.
